title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce Architecture
description: Apache Hadoop MapReduce is a software framework for easily writing applications which process vast amounts of data (multi-terabyte data-sets) parallelly on large clusters (thousands of nodes). It is designed to scale up from single servers to thousands of machines, each offering local computation and storage. The system works on a large cluster of computers using simple programming models. It provides a software framework for easily writing applications which process vast amounts of data (multi-terabyte data-sets) parallelly on large clusters (thousands of nodes). It is designed to scale up from single servers to thousands of machines, each offering local computation and storage. The system works on a large cluster of computers using simple programming models. It provides fault-tolerant distributed data-parallel processing of large data sets across clusters of computers using simple programming models.

Apache Hadoop MapReduce: Cluster org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.join.ss
description: This cluster is part of the Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework and provides functionality for performing joins over a set of data sources. The main components of this cluster are the ArrayListBackedIterator, ComposableInputFormat, ComposableRecordReader, CompositeInputFormat, CompositeInputSplit, CompositeRecordReader, InnerJoinRecordReader, JoinRecordReader, MultiFilterRecordReader, OuterJoinRecordReader, OverrideRecordReader, ResetableIterator, and StreamBackedIterator. These components interact with each other to read data, perform joins, and manage the input splits. The cluster achieves scalability, maintainability, fault-tolerance, and security by using the Apache Hadoop framework and its associated technologies. The technologies and frameworks used include Java, Hadoop, YARN, Avro, and Protocol Buffers. This cluster plays a crucial role in the overall MapReduce system by enabling efficient and scalable processing of large datasets across clusters of computers.

title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce: Cluster org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.util.ss
description: This cluster contains classes related to the Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework, which is a software framework for easily writing applications which process vast amounts of data (multi-terabyte data-sets) parallelly on large clusters (thousands of nodes). The classes in this cluster provide functionality for configuring and running MapReduce jobs, managing job submission and execution, and handling input/output data. The cluster includes classes for job configuration, cluster-level configuration, job limits, user-defined partitioning, field selection, key/value record reading, input formats, and more. The classes in this cluster are essential for building and running MapReduce applications on the Apache Hadoop platform.

title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce Architecture
description: Apache Hadoop MapReduce is a distributed processing framework that is used for processing large datasets in parallel. It is built on top of the Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) and YARN (Yet Another Resource Negotiator) resource management system. The MapReduce framework consists of two main components: the Map phase and the Reduce phase. The Map phase is responsible for dividing the input data into smaller chunks and mapping them to a set of intermediate key-value pairs. The Reduce phase is responsible for aggregating the intermediate data and producing the final output.

title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce Cluster
description: The Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework provides a cluster that allows access to information about the map/reduce cluster. It has methods to get the status of the JobTracker, get the system directory, get the staging area directory, get the job history directory, get the file system, and get the client protocol. It also has a method to close the cluster. The code uses the ClientProtocolProvider interface to create a client protocol for communication with the JobTracker. It initializes the client protocol by iterating through the ClientProtocolProvider implementations and choosing the first one that returns a non-null client protocol. If no implementation returns a non-null client protocol, an IOException is thrown. The code also includes imports for various classes and interfaces from the Apache Hadoop framework, such as Configuration, FileSystem, Path, UserGroupInformation, and ClientProtocolProvider. The code is part of the Apache Hadoop project and is licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0.

title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce - Cluster org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.task.ss
description: This cluster contains classes related to the execution of MapReduce tasks in Apache Hadoop. The main components include the Map and Reduce tasks, which are responsible for processing the input data and generating the output data. The cluster also includes classes for handling the communication between the tasks, managing the task status, and coordinating the execution of the tasks. The quality attributes achieved by this cluster include scalability, maintainability, fault-tolerance, and security. The technologies and frameworks used include Java, Hadoop, YARN, Avro, and Protocol Buffers. This cluster plays a crucial role in the overall MapReduce system by ensuring the efficient and reliable execution of MapReduce tasks.

title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce: Cluster org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.committer.manifest.ss
description: This cluster consists of classes related to the Apache Hadoop MapReduce library, specifically those in the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.committer.manifest.ss package. These classes are responsible for handling the commit process of MapReduce jobs and tasks, ensuring that the commit operations are performed successfully and efficiently. The classes in this cluster interact with the FileSystem API to perform file operations and with other classes and packages to manage the commit process. The cluster achieves scalability, maintainability, fault-tolerance, and security by using thread pools for asynchronous execution, logging and statistics for monitoring, and validation of output data. The technologies and frameworks used include Java, Hadoop, YARN, Avro, and Protocol Buffers. The cluster plays a crucial role in the overall MapReduce system by managing the commit process and ensuring the integrity and consistency of data.

Apache Hadoop MapReduce Checkpoint Service
description: The Apache Hadoop MapReduce Checkpoint Service is a distributed system that provides fault-tolerant and scalable checkpointing for MapReduce jobs. It is built on top of the Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) and uses the YARN resource manager to manage resources across the cluster. The Checkpoint Service provides a simple API for storing and retrieving task state, and it supports atomic, single-writer, write-once, multiple-reader objects. The Checkpoint Service is designed to be highly available, fault-tolerant, and scalable, and it integrates seamlessly with the MapReduce framework. It is used to ensure that MapReduce jobs can resume from failures and continue processing data from where they left off, without losing any progress.

title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce Output Format Cluster
description: The Apache Hadoop MapReduce Output Format Cluster is responsible for describing the output-specification for a Map-Reduce job. It provides methods for validating the output-specification, providing a RecordWriter implementation, and getting the output committer for the job. The cluster includes the OutputFormat class, which is an implementation of the OutputCommitter interface that commits files specified in the job output directory. The FileOutputCommitter class is used to handle the commit process, including creating and deleting temporary directories, as well as handling successful job output directories. The FileOutputCommitterFactory class is used to create instances of the FileOutputCommitter, which is used to commit the output of a MapReduce job. The LazyOutputFormat class is used to create output lazily, while the MapFileOutputFormat class is used to write MapFile objects. The MultipleOutputs class is a utility for writing output data to multiple outputs in a Hadoop MapReduce job.

title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce DB Input Format
description: The Apache Hadoop MapReduce DB Input Format is a component of the Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework that provides functionality for reading data from a database using MapReduce. It includes the DBInputFormat class, which emits LongWritable keys containing record numbers and DBWritable values, and the DBRecordReader class, which is a RecordReader that implements the initialize, close, getCurrentKey, getCurrentValue, createValue, getPos, next, getProgress, and nextKeyValue methods. The DBInputFormat class uses the DBSplitter interface to generate DBInputSplits for the DataDrivenDBInputFormat, which is used for reading data from a database using MapReduce. The DBSplitter interface has a single method, split, which takes a Configuration object, a ResultSet, and a column name as input and returns a list of InputSplit objects. The DBWritable interface is used for objects that are read from or written to a database, and provides implementations for writing the object's fields to a PreparedStatement and reading the fields from a ResultSet.

title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce Counters
description: The Apache Hadoop MapReduce Counters cluster is responsible for managing and tracking various counters related to file systems, resources, and application execution. It provides a robust and efficient framework for managing counters and includes functionality for adding, finding, and iterating over counter groups. The cluster is built using Java and is annotated with @InterfaceAudience.Private, indicating that it is intended for internal use within the Hadoop framework. The cluster achieves scalability, maintainability, fault-tolerance, and security by using the Apache Hadoop classification, io, mapreduce, and util packages. It also makes use of the Google Guava library for collections and iterators. The cluster plays a crucial role in the overall MapReduce system by providing a centralized location for managing and tracking counters, which helps in monitoring and optimizing application execution.

description: Apache Hadoop MapReduce is a software framework for easily writing applications which process vast amounts of data (multi-terabyte data-sets) across clusters of computers using simple programming models. title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce: Semantic Summaries of Java Classes
description: This architectural cluster in Apache Hadoop MapReduce provides semantic summaries of Java classes for data aggregation. The main components include the DoubleValueSum, LongValueMax, LongValueMin, LongValueSum, StringValueMax, StringValueMin, UniqValueCount, and UserDefinedValueAggregatorDescriptor classes. These classes implement value aggregators for various data types, such as double, long, and string. The cluster achieves quality attributes such as scalability, maintainability, fault-tolerance, and security. title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce Cluster
description: The Apache Hadoop MapReduce Cluster is a distributed computing framework that allows for the processing of large data sets across clusters of computers. It is built on top of the Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) and YARN (Yet Another Resource Negotiator) resource management system. The MapReduce Cluster provides a scalable and fault-tolerant architecture for processing large data sets in parallel. It consists of a JobTracker and a set of TaskTrackers, which work together to schedule and execute tasks across the cluster. The JobTracker is responsible for managing the overall job execution, while the TaskTrackers execute the individual tasks assigned by the JobTracker. The MapReduce Cluster supports various quality attributes, including scalability, maintainability, fault-tolerance, and security. It uses Java as its programming language and leverages various technologies and frameworks, such as Hadoop, YARN, Avro, and Protocol Buffers, to achieve its functionality. The MapReduce Cluster plays a crucial role in the overall MapReduce system, providing a distributed and fault-tolerant architecture for processing large data sets in parallel.

title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce: Cluster org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.chain.ss
description: This cluster consists of classes related to handling inconsistencies between MapReduce Context Objects API between different versions of Hadoop. It includes a factory to create job context, task context, and map context objects. The code is licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0. The cluster also includes an implementation of the OutputCommitter class, which is used by the Map-Reduce framework to manage the commit of task output for a Map-Reduce job. The OutputCommitter class provides methods to handle the setup, cleanup, and committing of job and task output. The cluster also includes the RecordReader class, which is used to read key/value pairs from input data for the Mapper in the Hadoop MapReduce framework. The RecordReader class provides methods for initializing the reader, reading the next key/value pair, getting the current key and value, getting the progress of the record reader, and closing the reader. The RecordReader class also implements the Closeable interface to allow for resource cleanup.

title: SecureShuffleUtils and JobTokenSecretManager
description: These classes provide utilities for secure shuffle operations in Apache Hadoop MapReduce. SecureShuffleUtils includes methods for generating and verifying hashes, building messages from URLs or HttpServletRequest, and converting byte arrays to hexadecimal strings. JobTokenSecretManager is a SecretManager for job tokens that can cache generated job tokens. It includes methods for creating new passwords/secrets for job token identifiers, adding/removing job tokens from cache, and retrieving token passwords/secrets for jobIds or job token identifiers. These classes play a crucial role in ensuring secure shuffle operations in the overall MapReduce system.
